difference: raws shows compact operation list with 2-column layout. AWS CLI shows full man-page style with service description, API version, and detailed formatting. Both list all operations correctly.
Both return same error (InvalidParameterException) when called without required params. Error message and behavior match.

Missing required param: raws sends request to server and gets server-side ValidationException (exit 254). AWS CLI does client-side validation with ParamValidation error (exit 252). Different error messages and exit codes.

CRITICAL: (1) raws does not auto-generate ClientRequestToken (idempotency token) - requires explicit --client-request-token. AWS CLI auto-generates one. (2) raws ignores --region and sends to us-west-2 default instead of us-east-1. (3) raws includes null fields (e.g. ReplicationStatus: null) in output; AWS CLI omits them.
Missing required param: raws gets server-side ValidationException (exit 254). AWS CLI does client-side ParamValidation (exit 252). Different error messages and exit codes.
Missing required param: raws gets server-side ValidationException (exit 254). AWS CLI does client-side ParamValidation (exit 252). Different error messages and exit codes.

CRITICAL: raws ignores --region when service params are present, routes to us-west-2 default. Functionally works when targeting correct region. Output differences: (1) raws includes many null fields (Type, Description, KmsKeyId, RotationEnabled, etc.) that AWS CLI omits. (2) Timestamps in UTC (+00:00) vs AWS CLI local timezone (-07:00).
Output structure matches (RandomPassword key with string value). Password values differ as expected (random). JSON format, text format, and table format all match structurally.
raws includes null fields (ResourcePolicy: null) that AWS CLI omits. Also affected by the --region routing bug when service params are present.

Affected by --region routing bug. Functionally works when targeting correct region. Output differences: (1) raws includes SecretBinary: null that AWS CLI omits. (2) Timestamps in UTC (+00:00) vs local timezone.
Affected by --region routing bug. Functionally works when targeting correct region. Output differences: (1) raws includes NextToken: null that AWS CLI omits. (2) Timestamps in UTC vs local timezone.

JSON output structure matches and --region works correctly. Differences: (1) Timestamps in UTC (+00:00) vs local timezone (-07:00). (2) Text output: timestamp format differs. (3) Table output: raws uses horizontal layout vs AWS CLI vertical key-value for single items; SecretVersionsToStages serialized as JSON string in raws table.
